test_cases.test_case: Open Bulk Import page via Import Client button
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Clients page is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Import Client' button in the top-right of the Clients page
test_cases.expected_result: Bulk Import page is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Open Create Client wizard via Create Client button
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Clients page is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Create Client' button in the top-right of the Clients page
test_cases.expected_result: Create Client wizard opens

test_cases.test_case: Open a client's Detail page from Clients table
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Clients page is open
A client exists in the clients list
steps: 1. Locate the client row in the Clients table (use Search if needed)
2. Click the client's Name link in the table
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page is displayed for the selected client

test_cases.test_case: Download client Excel template on Bulk Import page
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Bulk Import page is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Download Template' button
test_cases.expected_result: Client Excel template download is triggered in the browser

test_cases.test_case: Upload client file to start import job
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Bulk Import page is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Upload File' button
2. Select a <valid client import file> in the OS file dialog and confirm upload
test_cases.expected_result: A new row appears in the Import History table for the uploaded file indicating the import job has started

test_cases.test_case: Download import result from import history
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Bulk Import page is open
An import history row exists with a downloadable result
steps: 1. In the Import History table, click the 'Download' action for the relevant import row
test_cases.expected_result: Import result file download is triggered in the browser

test_cases.test_case: Submit Create Client wizard (happy path) and see Pending status
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Open the Create Client wizard from the Clients page by clicking 'Create Client'
2. In Step 1 (General Details), enter values for required fields: select <Office>, enter <First Name>, <Last Name>, and <Submitted On>; optionally enter <External ID> that is unique
3. Click Next to advance through Step 2 (Address Details) and Step 3 (Family Members) without adding optional entries
4. In Step 4 (Identifiers) either leave empty or add unique identifier entries
5. Click 'Submit' on the wizard
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page opens and the Status badge displays 'Pending' (yellow)

test_cases.test_case: Activate a Pending client with valid Activation Date
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Pending status with Submitted On = <submitted date>
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Activate' action in the action bar
2. In the Activate dialog, enter an <Activation_Date> that is on or after <submitted date>
3. Click Confirm on the Activate dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Status badge updates to 'Active' (green) on the Client Detail page

test_cases.test_case: Open Edit Client form for a Pending client
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Pending status
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit' action in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: Edit Client form is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Reject a Pending client with required reason
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Pending status
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Reject' action in the action bar
2. In the Reject dialog, enter a <Reason>
3. Click Confirm on the Reject dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Status badge updates to 'Rejected' (red) on the Client Detail page

test_cases.test_case: Withdraw a Pending client with required reason
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Pending status
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Withdraw' action in the action bar
2. In the Withdraw dialog, enter a <Reason>
3. Click Confirm on the Withdraw dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Status badge updates to 'Withdrawn' (orange) on the Client Detail page

test_cases.test_case: Open Edit Client form for an Active client
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Active status
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit' action in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: Edit Client form is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Transfer an Active client to a different office
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Active status and is assigned to <current office>
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Transfer Client' action in the action bar
2. In the Transfer dialog, select a <Destination_Office> that is different from <current office>
3. Click Confirm on the Transfer dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Office on the Client Detail page updates to display <Destination_Office>

test_cases.test_case: Close an Active client with no active accounts
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Active status with no active accounts
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Close' action in the action bar
2. In the Close dialog, enter a <Closure_Reason>
3. Click Confirm on the Close dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Status badge updates to 'Closed' (gray) on the Client Detail page

test_cases.test_case: Open Add Charge form for an Active client
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Active status
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Add Charge' action in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: Add Charge form is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Start New Loan wizard for an Active client
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Active status
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'New Loan' action in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: New Loan wizard opens

test_cases.test_case: Start New Savings wizard for an Active client
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Active status
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'New Savings' action in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: New Savings wizard opens

test_cases.test_case: Start New Share Account wizard for an Active client
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Active status
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'New Share Account' action in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: New Share Account wizard opens

test_cases.test_case: Reactivate a Closed client
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
A client exists in Closed status
Client Detail page for that client is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Reactivate' action in the action bar
2. Confirm the reactivate action in the confirmation dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Status badge updates to 'Active' (green) on the Client Detail page

test_cases.test_case: Search and filter clients list by status
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Clients page is open
Clients list contains multiple clients with different statuses
steps: 1. Enter <search term> in the Search field (searches Name / Account No. / External ID / Mobile Number) and execute the search
2. Select 'Active' from the Filter By Status dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Clients table displays only rows matching <search term> and having Status 'Active'; unrelated rows are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Create client and add unique Identifiers in wizard (no duplicate Document Type+Key)
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Open the Create Client wizard from the Clients page by clicking 'Create Client'
2. In Step 1 (General Details), enter values for required fields: select <Office>, enter <First Name>, <Last Name>, and <Submitted On>
3. Click Next to proceed to Step 2 and Step 3 without changes
4. In Step 4 (Identifiers), click 'Add Row' and enter <Document_Type> and <Document_Key> for the first identifier
5. Click 'Add Row' again and enter a different <Document_Type> and/or <Document_Key> so the pair is unique
6. Click 'Submit' on the wizard
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page opens and the Identifiers tab displays both added identifier entries (each with its Document Type and Document Key)

test_cases.test_case: Create Client — leave required text field (Office) blank
preconditions: User is logged in
Create Client wizard is open
steps: 1. Navigate to Create Client wizard
2. Leave the <Office> field blank
3. Fill all other required fields with valid values
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Office field indicating it is required; form does not submit; no client is created (status remains unchanged).

test_cases.test_case: Create Client — leave required date field (Submitted On) blank
preconditions: User is logged in
Create Client wizard is open
steps: 1. Navigate to Create Client wizard
2. Leave the <Submitted_On> field blank
3. Fill all other required fields with valid values
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Submitted On field indicating it is required; form does not submit; no client is created.

test_cases.test_case: Create Client — submit with duplicate External ID (uniqueness constraint)
preconditions: User is logged in
A client already exists with External ID = <duplicate External_ID>
Create Client wizard is open
steps: 1. Navigate to Create Client wizard
2. Enter <duplicate External_ID> in External ID
3. Fill all required fields with valid values
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: External ID field displays an error: 'must be unique across clients'; form does not submit; no client is created.

test_cases.test_case: Create Client — add duplicate Identifier (Document Type + Document Key) in Identifiers step
preconditions: User is logged in
Create Client wizard is open on Step 4 (Identifiers)
steps: 1. In Identifiers step, add an identifier with <Document_Type_1> and <Document_Key_1>
2. Add a second identifier item with the same <Document_Type_1> and <Document_Key_1>
3. Fill all required fields in the wizard
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Identifiers repeater displays an error: 'no duplicate Document Type + Document Key' for the duplicated entry; form does not submit; no client is created.

test_cases.test_case: Activate Pending client — leave Activation Date blank
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Pending status
Client Detail page for that Pending client is open
steps: 1. On Pending client's Detail page, click the Activate action
2. Leave the <Activation_Date> field blank in the Activate dialog
3. Click Confirm/Submit in the Activate dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Activation Date field indicating it is required; activation dialog does not submit; client remains in Pending status.

test_cases.test_case: Activate Pending client — enter Activation Date before Submitted On (constraint violation)
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Pending status with Submitted On = <submitted_on_date>
Client Detail page for that Pending client is open
steps: 1. On Pending client's Detail page, click the Activate action
2. Enter <activation date before submitted_on_date> in Activation Date
3. Click Confirm/Submit in the Activate dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Activation Date field displays an error: 'Activation Date must not be before Submitted On'; activation dialog does not submit; client remains in Pending status.

test_cases.test_case: Reject Pending client — leave Reason blank
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Pending status
Client Detail page for that Pending client is open
steps: 1. On Pending client's Detail page, click the Reject action
2. Leave the <Reason> field blank in the Reject dialog
3. Click Confirm/Submit in the Reject dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Reason field indicating it is required; reject dialog does not submit; client remains in Pending status.

test_cases.test_case: Withdraw Pending client — leave Reason blank
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Pending status
Client Detail page for that Pending client is open
steps: 1. On Pending client's Detail page, click the Withdraw action
2. Leave the <Reason> field blank in the Withdraw dialog
3. Click Confirm/Submit in the Withdraw dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Reason field indicating it is required; withdraw dialog does not submit; client remains in Pending status.

test_cases.test_case: Transfer Active client — leave Destination Office blank
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Active status
Client Detail page for that Active client is open
steps: 1. On Active client's Detail page, click the Transfer Client action
2. Leave the <Destination_Office> field blank in the Transfer dialog
3. Click Confirm/Submit in the Transfer dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Destination Office field indicating it is required; transfer dialog does not submit; client's Office remains unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Transfer Active client — select same office as destination (constraint violation)
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Active status with current Office = <current_office>
Client Detail page for that Active client is open
steps: 1. On Active client's Detail page, click the Transfer Client action
2. Select <current_office> as the Destination Office
3. Click Confirm/Submit in the Transfer dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Destination Office field displays an error: 'destination office must be different from current office'; transfer dialog does not submit; client's Office remains <current_office>.

test_cases.test_case: Close Active client — attempt to close when client has active accounts (precondition violation)
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Active status and has at least one active account
Client Detail page for that Active client is open
steps: 1. On Active client's Detail page, click the Close action
2. Enter a valid <Closure_Reason> in the Close dialog
3. Click Confirm/Submit in the Close dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Close action is blocked: an error is shown indicating the client cannot be closed due to active accounts (constraint: 'cannot close when client has active accounts' / precondition 'client must have no active accounts'); close does not complete; client remains in Active status.

test_cases.test_case: Close Active client — Close action should be unavailable when client has active accounts (precondition timing)
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Active status and has at least one active account
Client Detail page for that Active client is open
steps: 1. Open the Client Detail page for the Active client that has active accounts
2. Observe the available actions in the action bar before clicking anything
test_cases.expected_result: Close action button is not available (not visible or disabled) when the client has active accounts; the UI prevents initiating Close due to precondition 'client must have no active accounts'; client remains in Active status.

test_cases.test_case: Wrong-state attempt — on Pending client the Transfer Client action is not available
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Pending status
Client Detail page for that Pending client is open
steps: 1. Open the Pending client's Detail page
2. Look for a Transfer Client action/button in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer Client action is not visible in the action bar for a Pending client; action cannot be performed; client remains in Pending status.

test_cases.test_case: Wrong-state attempt — on Active client the Activate action is not available
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Active status
Client Detail page for that Active client is open
steps: 1. Open the Active client's Detail page
2. Look for an Activate action/button in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: Activate action is not visible in the action bar for an Active client; action cannot be performed; client remains in Active status.

test_cases.test_case: Wrong-state attempt — on Closed client Edit action is not available
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Closed status
Client Detail page for that Closed client is open
steps: 1. Open the Closed client's Detail page
2. Look for an Edit action/button in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: Edit action is not visible in the action bar for a Closed client; action cannot be performed; client remains in Closed status.

test_cases.test_case: Wrong-state check — Rejected and Withdrawn clients show no actions
preconditions: User is logged in
An existing client is in Rejected status and another client is in Withdrawn status
steps: 1. Open the Rejected client's Detail page
2. Observe the action bar for available actions
3. Open the Withdrawn client's Detail page
4. Observe the action bar for available actions
test_cases.expected_result: For both Rejected and Withdrawn clients the action bar shows no actionable buttons; no actions can be initiated; statuses remain Rejected and Withdrawn respectively.

test_cases.test_case: Activate: Activation Date equals Submitted On (boundary pass)
preconditions: A client exists in state Pending with a known Submitted_On date
steps: 1. Open the Clients page
2. Click the client's Name to open the Client Detail page for the Pending client
3. Click the Activate action
4. In the Activation_Date field enter a date exactly equal to the client's Submitted_On
5. Click Confirm on the Activate dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Activation action succeeds; client status changes to Active and the Client Detail page displays Activation Date equal to Submitted_On

test_cases.test_case: Activate: Activation Date one day before Submitted On (boundary fail)
preconditions: A client exists in state Pending with a known Submitted_On date
steps: 1. Open the Clients page
2. Click the client's Name to open the Client Detail page for the Pending client
3. Click the Activate action
4. In the Activation_Date field enter a date equal to one day before the client's Submitted_On
5. Click Confirm on the Activate dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked / error shown; an inline error is displayed on Activation_Date stating 'Activation Date must not be before Submitted On'

test_cases.test_case: Transfer Client: Destination_Office different from current office (boundary pass)
preconditions: A client exists in state Active with Current Office = <Office A>
steps: 1. Open the Clients page
2. Click the client's Name to open the Client Detail page for the Active client
3. Click the Transfer Client action
4. In the Destination_Office field select an office different from the client's Current Office
5. Click Submit on the Transfer dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer action succeeds; client's Office shown on the Client Detail page is updated to the chosen Destination_Office

test_cases.test_case: Transfer Client: Destination_Office same as current office (boundary fail)
preconditions: A client exists in state Active with Current Office = <Office A>
steps: 1. Open the Clients page
2. Click the client's Name to open the Client Detail page for the Active client
3. Click the Transfer Client action
4. In the Destination_Office field select the same office as the client's Current Office
5. Click Submit on the Transfer dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked / error shown; an inline error is displayed stating 'destination office must be different from current office'

test_cases.test_case: Create Client wizard: add then remove all Identifiers and submit
preconditions: User has opened Create Client wizard and has filled all required fields in earlier steps (Step 1 and Step 2) so the wizard is ready to submit
steps: 1. Navigate to Step 4 (Identifiers) in the Create Client wizard
2. Click Add Identifier to create the first Identifier row
3. Fill fields for the first Identifier row (Document_Type and Document_Key) as needed
4. Click Add Identifier to create a second Identifier row
5. Fill fields for the second Identifier row (Document_Type and Document_Key) as needed
6. For the first Identifier row click Remove/Delete to remove it
7. For the second Identifier row click Remove/Delete to remove it
8. Click Submit on the Create Client wizard
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission succeeds; client is created in Pending status and the Client Detail page (Identifiers tab) shows zero Identifier entries

test_cases.test_case: Reject Pending client with a very long Reason text
preconditions: A client exists in state Pending
steps: 1. Open the Clients page
2. Click the client's Name to open the Client Detail page for the Pending client
3. Click the Reject action
4. In the Reason field enter a very long text (200+ characters)
5. Click Confirm on the Reject dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Reject action succeeds; client status changes to Rejected and the Client Detail activity/history shows the entered Reason text (no inline error about length)

test_cases.test_case: Create Client: leading and trailing whitespace in First Name is trimmed on save
preconditions: User has opened Create Client wizard
steps: 1. In Step 1 enter First_Name with leading and trailing whitespace
2. Fill all other required fields in Step 1 and subsequent required steps (precondition allowed these to be filled)
3. Click Submit to create the client
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; Client Detail page displays the client's First Name without leading or trailing whitespace (value is trimmed)

test_cases.test_case: Create Client: rapid re-submission after successful submit does not create duplicate client
preconditions: User has completed the Create Client wizard required fields and is ready to submit
steps: 1. Click Submit on the Create Client wizard
2. After the system redirects to the newly created Client Detail page, press the browser Back button
3. On the page shown after Back attempt to click Submit again
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission is blocked / error shown; only one client record exists (no duplicate client is created) and the user remains on or is redirected to the existing client's detail page
